feedback.text: Hello, I'd like to give feedback regarding an ongoing issue related to digital purchases and more specifically video games. The issue being intentional planned obsolescence of video games and restriction of access to content you have already paid for. As an example I can refer to a game called Operation Flashpoint: Red River, developed by Codemasters, published by Electronic Arts back in 2011. As an older game and one in the niche genre of tactical shooters it is currently unplayable and support was silently cut off by EA. The game was removed from digital platforms (like Steam). The official website is dead http://www.flashpointgame.com/ and the only way to play it (on PC or console) is if you have a physical copy and find a way to circumvent the DRM (Games for Windows Live) which no longer works. This is just one example and there are thousands out there. As consumers we need more protection against such practices as they have been getting worse over the last decade, mainly attributed to the rise in popularity of videogames as media and the transition of game making from art to business. Thank you for your time and consideration. Best regards, Yanko
